steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best illustrates the claim that a species can be entirely absent at particular times of the year. The best example shows zero observations across multiple distinct time points.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'one month of zero observations': Shows absence at a single time point. This illustrates absence at one moment, not a recurring seasonal pattern. ✗
  • Choice B — 'one month of zero observations': Same issue as A — only one time point of absence, too narrow to show a seasonal pattern. ✗
  • Choice C — 'zero three-lined rainbowfish in January, April, and July of 2001': Three separate months all showing zero observations. This directly illustrates absence at particular times of the year with multiple data points. ✓
  • Choice D — 'one month of zero observations': Again, only a single time point. Doesn't establish a seasonal pattern. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
